title: Auto-clear all form fields on page load for fresh data entry

requirements.summary: Reset all form fields to blank/empty defaults automatically when each form/modal mounts for new entry, ensuring no pre-populated values appear while preserving the ability to edit existing records
acceptanceCriteria: Opening PurchaseOrderFormModal for a new entry shows all fields blank with no pre-populated date, supplier, fuel type, invoice number, or quantity values.
Opening RecordSaleModal shows all fields blank with no pre-populated fuel type, quantity, payment method, pump number, or staff values.
Opening SupplierFormModal for a new supplier shows all fields blank.
Opening StaffFormModal for a new staff member shows all fields blank including hire date and salary.
Opening RegisterCustomerModal for a new customer shows all fields blank.
Opening ShiftFormModal shows all fields blank including date, start/end time, and pump number.
Expense entry form opens with all fields blank.
Existing saved records in the store and database are unaffected after forms are reset.
Edit forms (when opened for an existing record) still correctly populate with that record's data — only 'new entry' forms open blank.

file_operations.description: Modify form state initialization to reset all fields (date, supplier, fuelType, invoiceNumber, totalLitres, pricePerLitre, orderDate, deliveryDate, status) to empty/default values when the modal opens for a new purchase order. Ensure the useEffect or initialization logic clears form state on mount when no existing order is being edited.

Modify form state initialization to reset all fields (fuelType, quantity, paymentMethod, pumpNumber, staffId, totalAmount) to empty/default values when the modal opens. Add a useEffect hook that runs on mount to clear form state, ensuring no pre-populated values appear for new sale entries.

Modify form state initialization to reset all fields (name, contact, email, address, fuelTypes array) to empty/default values when the modal opens for a new supplier. Ensure the component distinguishes between add mode (blank fields) and edit mode (pre-populated with existing supplier data).

Modify form state initialization to reset all fields (name, role, contact, hireDate, status, salary amount, salary payPeriod) to empty/default values when the modal opens for a new staff member. Add logic to detect whether a staff member is being edited (pre-populate) or added new (blank fields).

Modify form state initialization to reset all fields (name, contact, vehicleNumber) to empty values when the modal opens for a new customer. Ensure edit mode still pre-populates fields with the selected customer's data while new registrations start with blank inputs.

Modify form state initialization to reset all fields (staffId, date, startTime, endTime, pumpNumber, status) to empty/default values when the modal opens for a new shift. Add a useEffect that clears form state on mount to ensure fresh data entry.

Modify the expense entry form/modal state initialization to reset all expense fields (category, description, amount, date, etc.) to empty/default values when the form opens for a new expense entry. Ensure the form clears on mount and does not retain previous values.
